title: Halle Bailey Says Racist ‘Little Mermaid’ Backlash ‘Taught Me How to Block Out the Noise’
content:
ByZack Sharf

Digital News Director

Three years after the release of Disney’s live-action “The Little Mermaid,”Halle Baileyis looking back at the experience of playing Ariel and how she ultimately felt freed by the discourse that erupted around her casting. Racist trolls were outraged at Disney for casting a Black actor as Ariel, which made Bailey the target of online harassment from toxic fans. But she doesn’t look back at the film’s release with any negativity.

Speaking toThe Independenton the press tour for her latest film, the romantic comedy “You, Me & Tuscany,” Bailey said “The Little Mermaid” was “a beautiful experience for me – and I feel like it taught me to listen to myself and the good voices inside. I learned how to block out the noise.”

“How do I explain it…” she continued. “It was actually freeing to be in the middle of this conversation where so many different opinions were coming in, and they were so opposite from one another… I felt like I was watching myself inside a cup, seeing how people react to it… Growing up in the industry can really develop your sense of self, and for me, it keeps me grounded in a way. I know for some people it’s the opposite but I just always think to myself, ‘None of this is real.’”

Bailey explained that she often goes out into nature to get the perspective she needs to stay levelheaded in Hollywood, saying: “I love feeling small, realizing that the world is so big and beautiful and I’m just a tiny, tiny part of it. The fact I’m here is a blessing, and I’m grateful [to be doing music and acting], but at the same time, this is not what matters in life. What matters is keeping our feet on the ground, and holding the people we love.”

A few months before “The Little Mermaid” opened in theaters in May 2023, Bailey toldThe Facethat “as a Black person you just expect” the kind of racist backlash she endured for being cast as Ariel and “it’s not really a shock anymore.”

”When [Chlöe and I] first signed to Parkwood, [Beyoncé] was always like: ​’I never read my comments. Don’t ever read the comments,’” she added at the time. “Honestly, when the teaser came out, I was at the D23 Expo and I was so happy. I didn’t see any of the negativity. I know people are like: ​’It’s not about race.’ But now that I’m her…People don’t understand that when you’re Black there’s this whole other community. It’s so important for us to see ourselves.”

Bailey had previouslytoldVarietythat her family helped her drown out the backlash when the #NotMyAriel hashtag started trending on X. Bailey’s grandparents shared their own memories with her of the racism and discrimination they faced in their lifetimes.

“It was an inspiring and beautiful thing to hear their words of encouragement, telling me, ‘You don’t understand what this is doing for us, for our community, for all the little Black and brown girls who are going to see themselves in you,’” she said.

“The Little Mermaid” director Rob Marshall later toldEntertainment Weeklythat he had “no agenda” when it came to the casting of Ariel and that his team auditioned “every ethnicity” for the role before landing on Bailey.

“We just were looking for the best actor for the role, period. The end,” Marshall said. “We saw everybody and every ethnicity. [The goal was to find someone who can be] incredibly strong, passionate, beautiful, smart, clever [and with] a great deal of fire and joy.”

Head over toThe Independent’s websiteto read Bailey’s profile in its entirety.
title_zh: 哈莉·貝莉(Halle Bailey)表示，對於《小美人魚》(The Little Mermaid)的種族主義反對聲音讓她學會了如何屏蔽噪音。
content_zh:
撰文：扎克·沙夫(Zack Sharf)

數位新聞總監

在迪士尼真人版《小美人魚》(The Little Mermaid) 發行三年後，哈莉·貝莉(Halle Bailey) 回顧了她飾演艾莉兒(Ariel) 的經歷，以及她如何最終感受到被圍繞在她選角的討論中所釋放。種族主義者對迪士尼選擇一位黑人演員飾演艾莉兒感到憤怒，這使得貝莉成為有毒粉絲的網絡騷擾目標。但她並不以任何負面情緒回顧這部電影的發行。

在為她最新的浪漫喜劇《你我與托斯卡尼》(You, Me & Tuscany) 進行的新聞發布會上，貝莉表示《小美人魚》對她來說是一段“美好的經歷——我覺得這教會了我傾聽自己和內心的好聲音。我學會了如何屏蔽噪音。”

“我該怎麼解釋呢……”她接著說。“能夠身處這場充滿不同意見的對話中，實際上是釋放的，因為這些意見彼此之間是如此對立……我感覺自己就像在一個杯子裡看著自己，觀察人們的反應……在這個行業中成長真的能夠發展你的自我意識，對我來說，這讓我以某種方式保持腳踏實地。我知道對某些人來說正好相反，但我總是告訴自己，‘這一切都不是真實的。’”

貝莉解釋說，她經常走進大自然，以獲得在好萊塢保持冷靜所需的視角，她說：“我喜歡感受到渺小，意識到這個世界是如此之大而美麗，而我只是其中一個微小的部分。我能在這裡是一種祝福，我對於能夠從事音樂和演藝事業感到感激，但同時，這並不是生活中最重要的。重要的是保持腳踏實地，珍惜我們所愛的人。”

在《小美人魚》於2023年5月上映前幾個月，貝莉告訴《面孔》(The Face)，“作為一名黑人，你只是期望”因為被選為艾莉兒而遭受的種族主義反彈，“這已經不再是震驚的事情了。”

“當[克洛伊(Chlöe)和我]第一次簽約到Parkwood時，[碧昂絲(Beyoncé)]總是說：‘我從不看我的評論。永遠不要看評論，’”她當時補充道。“老實說，當預告片發布時，我在D23博覽會上，我感到非常高興。我沒有看到任何負面的反應。我知道人們會說：‘這與種族無關。’但現在我身在這裡……人們不明白，當你是黑人時，這裡有一整個不同的社群。對我們來說，看到自己是如此重要。”

貝莉之前告訴《綜藝》(Variety)，當#NotMyAriel標籤開始在X平台上流行時，她的家人幫助她抵擋了這些反彈。貝莉的祖父母與她分享了他們一生中面對的種族主義和歧視的回憶。

“聽到他們的鼓勵之詞，告訴我，‘你不明白這對我們、對我們的社區、對所有將在你身上看到自己的小黑人和棕色女孩有多重要，’這是一件鼓舞人心而美好的事情，”她說。

《小美人魚》(The Little Mermaid) 的導演羅伯·馬歇爾(Rob Marshall)後來告訴《娛樂週刊》(Entertainment Weekly)，他在選角艾莉兒(Ariel)時“沒有任何議程”，並且他的團隊為這個角色試鏡了“每一種族裔”，最終選擇了貝莉(Bailey)。

“我們只是尋找這個角色的最佳演員，僅此而已。”馬歇爾說。“我們看過每個人和每一種族裔。[目標是找到一個] 極其強大、充滿熱情、美麗、聰明、機智，並且擁有大量火焰和喜悅的人。”

請前往《獨立報》(The Independent) 的網站，閱讀貝莉的完整個人資料。
summary_zh:
撰文：扎克·沙夫(Zack Sharf)

數位新聞總監

在迪士尼真人版《小美人魚》(The Little Mermaid) 發行三年後，哈莉·貝莉(Halle Bailey) 回顧了她飾演艾莉兒(Ariel) 的經歷，以及她如何最終感受到被圍繞在她選角的討論中所釋放。種族主義者對迪士尼選擇一位黑人演員飾演艾莉兒感到憤怒，這使得貝莉成為有毒粉絲的網絡騷擾目標。
